page: Amazon Web Services m9gd.medium is a general-purpose instance powered by a single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 core based on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture. Operating at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, this processor features 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 48 MB of L3 cache. The instance is equipped with 4.0 GB of system memory, providing a 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. Local storage is bundled via a 59 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume, and the network interface supports a baseline bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark results highlight strong single-threaded execution in specific tasks alongside expected constraints for a single-core configuration. The instance achieves top-tier performance in single-threaded gzip compression at 36.23 GB/sec and bzip3 decompression at 17.04 GB/sec. However, multi-core stress-ng tests and Passmark CPU scores are low due to the single-vCPU limitation. Memory performance is mixed, with an average large-block read bandwidth of 42.93 GB/sec but lower write speeds of 13.9 GB/sec. Redis SET operations reach 3.31 million operations per second, and static web serving throughput is measured at 4.1 GB/sec.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the m9gd.medium offers a low-cost entry point to the Graviton9 family, combining dedicated ARMv9 compute resources with high-speed local NVMe storage. The primary tradeoff is the single-core architecture, which limits parallel processing capabilities and multi-threaded throughput. This instance is suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications, basic web serving, development environments, and small-scale caching or database tasks that benefit from local SSD storage but do not require high multi-core concurrency.
description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.medium is a general-purpose, single-core instance utilizing a dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 processor built on the ARMv9 arm64 architecture. Operating at 2.8 GHz, it features 4.0 GB of RAM and a bundled 59 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume. Network performance is rated at a 1 Gbps baseline. Benchmarks reveal top-tier single-threaded performance in gzip compression (36.23 GB/sec) and bzip3 decompression (17.04 GB/sec), though multi-core and memory write metrics remain low due to the single-vCPU constraint. The instance provides a cost-effective entry point for workloads requiring dedicated ARMv9 compute and local NVMe storage without high parallel processing demands. It is suited for lightweight web serving, single-threaded utilities, development environments, and small-scale caching.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.medium is a general-purpose instance featuring a single dedicated 2.8 GHz AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 ARMv9 core, 4.0 GB of RAM, and a 59 GB NVMe SSD instance store volume.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m9gd.medium is a single-core ARMv9 instance with 4.0 GB of RAM and a 59 GB NVMe SSD, designed for lightweight general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A single-core ARMv9 instance featuring dedicated compute, integrated NVMe SSD storage, and efficient single-threaded compression performance.
bullet_points: Single dedicated AWS Graviton Neoverse-V3 ARMv9 core running at 2.8 GHz
4.0 GB of system memory and 1 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
Bundled 59 GB local NVMe SSD instance store volume
Top-tier single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression speeds
Suited for lightweight web serving, development, and single-threaded tasks
